Knowledge Base

Hybrid Search

Mnemosyne's flagship retrieval capability — combining dense vector similarity with SQLite FTS5 full-text search for results that are both semantically relevant and precisely matched.

Why Hybrid?

ApproachStrengthsWeaknesses
Vector onlySemantic similarity, fuzzy matchingMisses exact terms, no boolean logic
Text onlyExact matching, boolean queriesNo semantic understanding, synonym blind
HybridBest of bothSlightly higher latency

Fusion Algorithm

final_score = (vector_score * vector_weight) + 
            (text_score * text_weight) + 
            (temporal_score * temporal_weight)

Default weights:

  • Vector: 0.5
  • Text: 0.3
  • Temporal: 0.2

Usage

# Default hybrid search
results = mem.recall("What database does the project use?")

# Custom weights
results = mem.recall(
  "What database does the project use?",
  vector_weight=0.3,   # Prefer exact text matches
  text_weight=0.6,
  temporal_weight=0.1,
)

# With filters
results = mem.recall(
  "database migration",
  tags=["backend", "infrastructure"],
  since="2026-04-01",
  top_k=5,
)

Result Format

[
  {
      "id": "mem_abc123",
      "content": "We decided to use PostgreSQL for the primary database...",
      "score": 0.92,
      "vector_score": 0.88,
      "text_score": 0.95,
      "source": "decision",
      "created_at": 1714060800,
  },
  ...
]

Performance

Query TypeMedian Latencyp99 Latency
Vector only35ms120ms
FTS5 only15ms45ms
Hybrid (default)65ms180ms
Hybrid + filters85ms250ms

Tuning Weights

Different use cases benefit from different weight configurations:

Use CaseVectorTextTemporal
Fact lookup0.20.70.1
Concept search0.70.20.1
Recent events0.30.30.4
General recall0.50.30.2
Index Requirements

Hybrid search requires both vector and FTS5 indices to be built. These are created automatically on first use. Initial index build may take a few seconds for large datasets.
